sectiondef.header: Register offsets for the WWDT core with Generic & windowing

briefdescription.para: Task Signature Register 0 Offset for WWDT.
Task Signature Register 1 Offset for WWDT.
Second Sequence Timer Register Offset for WWDT.
Second Sequence Timer Window Configuration Register Offset.
Generic Watchdog Control and Status Register Offset.
Generic Watchdog Compare Value Register 0 Offset.
Generic Watchdog Compare Value Register 1 Offset.
Generic Watchdog Warm Reset Register Offset.

sectiondef.header: Register offsets for the AXI Timebase WDT core. Each register is 32
Generic Watchdog Control and Status Register bits

briefdescription.para: Restart kick or disable attempt in first window.
This typedef contains enumeration of different events in basic mode.

memberdef.name: XWdtTb_ConfigTable

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This will generate the first kick and start first window. This auto clears MWC bit to make address space read only.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This will start the first window of generic Watchdog timer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCESS, if Generic WDT feature is disabled.
XST_FAILURE, if Generic WDT feature is not disabled.

simplesect.para: This will disable generic Watchdog Timer.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the AXI timebase Watchdog Timer core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the core. Initialization of the core includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Points to the configuration structure associated with the AXI timebase Watchdog Timer core.
Base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific legacy/window watchdog timer/timebase instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called before other functions of the driver are called.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
contains the base address of the device

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the configuration for device ID/ BaseAddress was not found

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Starts the legacy or window watchdog timer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: For legacy, the timebase is reset to 0 when the Watchdog Timer is started. The timebase is always incrementing.
For window, this generates first kick and starts the first window. This step auto clears MWC bit to make address space read only.

briefdescription.para: Disables the legacy or window watchdog timer.

detaileddescription.para: It is the caller's responsibility to disconnect the interrupt handler of the watchdog timer from the interrupt source, typically an interrupt controller, and disable the interrupt in the interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the legacy/window watchdog was stopped successfully.
XST_NO_FEATURE if disable is not supported in legacy watchdog timer.
XST_FAILURE if the window watchdog timer cannot be stopped.
For legacy, the hardware configuration controls this functionality. If it is not allowed by the hardware the failure will be returned and the timer will continue without interruption.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the legacy watchdog timer has expired or window watchdog timer either in second window or not in second window.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used for polled mode in legacy watchdog timer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE, if the legacy watchdog timer has expired or window watchdog timer is not in second window.
FALSE if the legacy watchdog is not expired or window watchdog is in second window.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the generic watchdog timer has first window expired or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE, if the generic watchdog timer first window has expired.
FALSE if the generic watchdog is not expired first window.

briefdescription.para: Restarts the legacy or window watchdog timer.

detaileddescription.para: An application needs to call this function periodically to keep the timer from asserting the reset output.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function keeps window Watchdog Timer always enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This must be called before Window WDT is enabled. Once Window WDT is enabled, it can only be disabled by applying reset.

briefdescription.para: Clears event(s) that present after system reset.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Event can be cleared by applying reset to the core followed by writing 111. Writing any other pattern has no effect.

briefdescription.para: Clears the window watchdog reset pending.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Clears window watchdog timer interrupt (WINT) bit.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Sets byte count to determine the interrupt assertion point in the second window configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Specifies the selected byte count value to be set in the second window configuration.

listitem.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.
This function must be used along with XWdtTb_SetByteSegment.

briefdescription.para: Provides byte count value of the selected byte count in the second window configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Byte count value of the selected byte count in the second window configuration.

briefdescription.para: Sets byte segment selection to determine the interrupt assertion point in the second window configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

Specifies the byte segment selected.
0 = Second window byte 0 selected.
1 = Second window byte 1 selected.
2 = Second window byte 2 selected.
3 = Second window byte 3 selected.

listitem.para: This function must be called before window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.
This function must be used along with XWdtTb_SetByteCount.

briefdescription.para: Provides byte segment selection in the second window configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para:
Byte segment selection value as
0 = Second window byte 0 selected.
1 = Second window byte 1 selected.
2 = Second window byte 2 selected.
3 = Second window byte 3 selected.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

SST provides additional time to software by delaying the inevitable window watchdog reset generation by SST count(SC) delay. This is an independent function and can be enabled in any mode with or without other options.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

PSM enables Task Signature Register comparison. When PSM is enabled, core checks and compares the contents of TSR0 and TSR1 registers at the restart kick/disablement of Window WDT in second window. If they match, no effect. If they do not match, reset is generated either immediately when SST is disabled or after SST count delay when SST is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.
When fail counter is enabled, reset is generated when fail counter is 7 and another bad event happens.
When fail counter is disabled, one bad event triggers reset either immediately when SST is disabled or after SST count delay when SST is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: Provides extra safeguard against unintentional clear of WEN bit.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: Enables unintentional clear of WEN bit.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: Sets the count value for the first and second window.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Specifies the first window count value.
Specifies the second window count value.

simplesect.para:
This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.
For first window, it is recommended that minimum non-zero value should be 15 or more.
For second window, minimum value should be sufficiently large to complete required AXILite write transactions at system level.
Setting second window count value to zero causes wrong configuration and disables Window WDT feature by clearing WEN bit irrespective of WDP settings.

briefdescription.para: Sets the count value for the GWDT_Compare_value_reg0 ,GWDT_Compare_value_reg1 & GWDT_Offset_regs .

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Specifies the GWDT_Offset_reg count value.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the count value for the GWDT_Offset_regs .

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
GWDT count value in milliseconds.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: Programs the width of Watchdog Timer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Width of the Watchdog Timer.

listitem.para: XST_SUCESS, if window mode is disabled and the width is programmed correctly.
XST_FAILURE, if Window mode is enabled or if the width is not in the range of 8-31
This function is applicable only when the window mode is disabled.
This function should be called before starting the timer. Valid values for the width are 8-31. Programming any other value returns failure.

briefdescription.para: This routine generates responses based on input token in QA mode.

parameterdescription.para: Token value read from TVAL
Byte on which response is generated
Token feedback value read from TFR

simplesect.para: Response byte (calculated based on logic given WWDT architecture)

briefdescription.para: Returns the current contents of the timebase.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Controls the read/write access to the complete Window WDT register space.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

Specifies a value that needs to be used to provide read/write access to the register space.
1 = Window WDT register space is writable.
0 = Window WDT register space is read only.

simplesect.para: When register space is set read only, writes to any register are ignored and does not lead to good or bad event generation.

briefdescription.para: Provides Window WDT register space read only or writable.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 = Window WDT register space is writable.
0 = Window WDT register space is read only.

briefdescription.para: Provides the last bad event and read even after system reset.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: One of the enumeration value described in XWdtTb_Event.
Event can be read after a system reset to determine if the reset was caused by a watchdog timeout.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Fail counter value. The default value is 5.

listitem.para: When fail counter is enabled, it gets incremented for every bad event unless 7 or decrement ed for every good event unless 0. If fail counter is 7 and another bad event happens, reset is generated.
When fail counter is disabled, bad event leads to reset.

briefdescription.para: States that whether the reset is pending or not when Secondary Sequence Timer(SST) counter has started.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 = window watchdog reset is pending.
0 = window watchdog reset is not pending.

briefdescription.para: States that whether window watchdog timer has reached at the interrupt programmed point in second window.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 = when window watchdog timer has reached at the interrupt programmed point in second window.
0 = when window watchdog timer did not reach at the interrupt programmed point in second window.

briefdescription.para: States wrong configuration when second window count is set to zero.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 = if second window count is set to zero.
0 = if second window count is not set to zero.

briefdescription.para: Sets the count value for the Second Sequence Timer window.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Specifies the first window count value.

simplesect.para:
This function must be called before Window WDT start/enable or after Window WDT stop/disable.
For SSTWR window, This option provides additional time to software by delaying the inevitable wwdt_reset assertion/generation by SC count delay

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Specifies the GWDT or WWDT. 0 for GWDT and 1 for WWDT.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCESS, if Mode with in the range. -XST_FAILURE,if Mode is out side of range.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Specifies the value to be written to token resp register.

briefdescription.para: Sets the first feedback value for the TFR .

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Feedback to be programmed.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT QA start/enable or after Window WDT QA stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: Reads the Token Feedback Register to return the feedback value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Feedback value programmed in TFR register.

briefdescription.para: Reads the ESR Register to return the token value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Sets the seed value for the TFR .

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Used for Input of Seed.

simplesect.para: This function must be called before Window WDT QA start/enable or after Window WDT QA stop/disable.

briefdescription.para: Returns true if the watchdog is in second window.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: TRUE if in second window, otherwise FALSE.

briefdescription.para: Updates the Token Resp Register (TRR) with the answer value provided from user.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.
Value to be programmed.

briefdescription.para: Reads the Answer byte count from the ESR register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Enables the Q&A mode by writing Func_Ctrl register .

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Disables the Q&A mode by writing Func_Ctrl register .

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XWdtTb_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xwdttb.h for the definition of XWdtTb_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the timebase or window if enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Timebase test verifies that the timebase is incrementing. The watchdog timer is not tested due to the time required to wait for the watchdog timer to expire. The time consumed by this test is dependent on the system clock and the configuration of the dividers in for the input clock of the timebase.
Window test verifies that the windowing feature does not generate bad event after enabling window feature with first and second window count. It disables window feature immediately.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self-test was successful.
XST_WDTTB_TIMER_FAILED if the timebase is not incrementing.
XST_FAILURE if self-test was failed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCESS, if Window WDT feature is disabled.
XST_FAILURE, if Window WDT feature is not disabled.
Disabling watchdog in first window duration is considered as bade event. It can only be disabled in the second window duration.
If fail counter is enabled, watchdog can be disabled only when fail counter is zero.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: This will start the timebase watchdog timer.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XWdtTb instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCESS, if Timebase WDT feature is disabled.
XST_FAILURE, if Timebase WDT feature is not disabled.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Enable Generic Watchdog Warm Reset Register.
This section contains the required functions of the XWdtTb driver.

detaileddescription.para: The xwdttb_sinit.c file contains static initialization method for AXI Timebase Window Watchdog Timer core.
The xwdttb_selftest.c file contains diagnostic self-test functions for the XWdtTb component.
The xwdttb_l.h header file contains identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
The xwdttb_hw.h header file contains identifiers and register-level core functions (or macros) that can be used to access the AXI Timebase and Window Watchdog Timer core.
The xwdttb_g.c file contains the required functions of the XWdtTb driver.
The xwdttb_config.h file contains the required functions of the XWdtTb driver.
The xwdttb_config.c file contains the required functions of the XWdtTb driver.
See xwdttb.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 08/16/01 First release 1.00b jhl 02/21/02 Repartitioned the driver for smaller files 1.00b rpm 04/26/02 Made LookupConfig public 1.10b mta 03/23/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL processor APIs/macros. 4.0 sha 12/17/15 Added Window WDT feature with basic mode. Moved XWdtTb_LookupConfig definition to xwdttb_sinit.c. Adherence to coding and Doxygen guidelines. Removed included xil_io, xil_types, xparameters and xil_assert header files. Moved XWdtTb_GetTbValue to xwdttb.h file. Adherence to MISRA-C guidelines. 4.0 sha 01/29/16 Added functions for Window WDT feature: XWdtTb_AlwaysEnable, XWdtTb_ClearLastEvent, XWdtTb_ClearResetPending, XWdtTb_IntrClear, XWdtTb_SetByteCount, XWdtTb_GetByteCount, XWdtTb_SetByteSegment, XWdtTb_GetByteSegment, XWdtTb_EnableSst, XWdtTb_DisableSst, XWdtTb_EnablePsm, XWdtTb_DisablePsm, XWdtTb_EnableFailCounter, XWdtTb_DisableFailCounter, XWdtTb_EnableExtraProtection, XWdtTb_DisableExtraProtection, XWdtTb_SetWindowCount, XWdtTb_EnableWinWdt, XWdtTb_DisableWinWdt, XWdtTb_CfgInitialize.
Updated functions with Window WDT feature: XWdtTb_Start, XWdtTb_Stop, XWdtTb_IsWdtExpired, XWdtTb_RestartWdt.

Modified lines with exceeding maximum 80 chars. Changed multi line comments to single line comments wherever required. 4.3 srm 01/27/18 Added XWdtTb_ProgramWDTWidth which can program the width of WDT 01/30/18 Added doxygen tags 4.4 aru 11/15/18 Replaced "Xil_AssertVoid" as "Xil_AssertNonvoid" in XWdtTb_ProgramWDTWidth(). 4.4 sne 02/28/19 Added Static functions for Window WDT feature: XWdtTb_EnableTiWdt,XWdtTb_DisableTiWdt. 4.4 sne 03/01/19 Fixed violations according to MISRAC-2012 standards modified the code for below violations, No brackets to then/else, Literal value requires a U suffix,Function return type inconsistent,Logical conjunctions need brackets, Declared the pointer param as Pointer to const, Procedure has more than one exit point. 4.4 sne 03/04/19 Added support for Versal( Generic Watchdog and Window Watchdog Timer). Added below functions: XWdtTb_EnableGenericWdt,XWdtTb_DisableGenericWdt, XWdtTb_EnableTimebaseWdt,XWdtTb_DisableTimebaseWdt, XWdtTb_IsGenericWdtFWExpired,XWdtTb_SetGenericWdtWindow. 4.5 sne 06/25/19 Fixed Coverity warning in driver file. 4.5 sne 09/27/19 Added common driver support for Window watchdog Timer and AXI Timebase watchdog timer. 5.0 sne 01/31/20 Removed compare value registers write in XWdtTb_SetGenericWdtWindow function. 5.0 sne 02/27/20 Reorganize the driver source and Fixed doxygen warnings. 5.0 sne 03/09/20 Fixed MISRA-C violations. 5.7 sb 07/12/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 5.7 sb 09/13/23 Fix MISRA-C violation 12.1. 5.9 ht 05/15/23 Port XWdtTb_Initialize() to SDT flow 5.12 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.

detaileddescription.para: See xwdttb.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
5.0 sne 11/19/19 First release 5.7 sb 09/13/23 Fix MISRA-C violation 10.4

detaileddescription.para: See xwdttb.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
5.0 sne 11/19/19 First release

detaileddescription.para: See xwdttb.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 08/16/01 First release 4.3 srm 01/30/18 Added doxygen tags 4.4 sne 03/04/19 Added support for versal 4.5 sne 09/27/19 Added common configuration structure for AXI Timebase WDT and WWWDT.

detaileddescription.para: For more information about the operation of this core see the hardware specification and documentation in the higher level driver xwdttb.h file.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 12/17/15 First release. 4.0 sha 01/29/16 Updated version to 4.0 as it is newly added file in driver version 4.0. Added following macros for Window WDT: XWT_MWR_OFFSET, XWT_ESR_OFFSET, XWT_FCR_OFFSET, XWT_FWR_OFFSET, XWT_SWR_OFFSET, XWT_TSR0_OFFSET, XWT_TSR1_OFFSET, XWT_STR_OFFSET.

Added following masks and shifts macros for Window WDT: XWT_MWR_AEN_MASK, XWT_MWR_MWC_MASK, XWT_ESR_LBE_MASK, XWT_ESR_FCV_MASK, XWT_ESR_WRP_MASK, XWT_ESR_WINT_MASK, XWT_ESR_WSW_MASK, XWT_ESR_WCFG_MASK, XWT_ESR_WEN_MASK, XWT_FCR_SBC_MASK, XWT_FCR_BSS_MASK, XWT_FCR_SSTE_MASK, XWT_FCR_PSME_MASK, XWT_FCR_FCE_MASK, XWT_FCR_WM_MASK, XWT_FCR_WDP_MASK.

XWT_ESR_LBE_SHIFT, XWT_ESR_FCV_SHIFT, XWT_ESR_WRP_SHIFT, XWT_ESR_WINT_SHIFT, XWT_ESR_WSW_SHIFT, XWT_ESR_WCFG_SHIFT, XWT_FCR_SBC_SHIFT, XWT_FCR_BSS_SHIFT, XWT_FCR_SSTE_SHIFT, XWT_FCR_WM_SHIFT. 4.3 srm 01/30/18 Added doxygen tags 4.4 sne 03/04/19 Added Support for Versal (Generic Watchdog and Window Watchdog Timer). Added following macros for Generic WDT: XWT_TFR_OFFSET,XWT_TRR_OFFSET,XWT_IENR_OFFSET, XWT_IDR_OFFSET,XWT_IMR_OFFSET,XWT_GWRR_OFFSET, XWT_GWCSR_OFFSET,XWT_GWOR_OFFSET,XWT_GWCVR0_OFFSET, XWT_GWCVR1_OFFSET,XWT_GW_WR_OFFSET,XWT_GWCSR_GWEN_MASK, XWT_GWCSR_GWS1_MASK,XWT_GWCSR_GWS2_MASK,XWT_GWCSR_MASK, XWT_GW_WR_MASK,XWT_GWRR_MASK. Added following macro for Win WDT: XWT_SSTWR_OFFSET. 4.5 sne 09/27/19 Added common driver support for WWDT and AXI Timebase WDT. Removed versal flag. Removed AXI Timebase WDT offsets. Added following macros for WWDT XWT_TSR0_WWDT_OFFSET,XWT_TSR1_WWDT_OFFSET, XWT_STR_WWDT_OFFSET. 5.0 sne 03/11/20 Added XWT_GWDT and XWT_WWDT macros.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b rpm 04/26/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/23/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 22/10/09 The following macros defined in this file have been removed - XWdtTb_mEnableWdt, XWdtTb_mDisbleWdt, XWdtTb_mRestartWdt XWdtTb_mGetTimebaseReg and XWdtTb_mHasReset. Added the XWdtTb_ReadReg and XWdtTb_WriteReg macros. User should XWdtTb_ReadReg/XWdtTb_WriteReg to achieve the desired functionality of the macros that were removed. 4.0 sha 12/17/15 Added Window WDT feature with basic mode. Removed extra include files. Moved constant and macro definitions to xwdttb_hw.h file. 4.3 srm 01/30/18 Added doxygen tags
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 02/06/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/23/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated to use the HAL processor APIs/macros. 4.0 sha 12/17/15 Adherence to coding and Doxygen guidelines. Changed int -> u32. Update self-test with window WDT feature. Removed included xil_types, xil_io and xil_assert header files. Adherence to MISRA-C guidelines. 4.0 sha 01/29/16 Added following macros for Window WDT feature: XWT_FW_COUNT, XWT_SW_COUNT. 4.3 srm 01/30/18 Added doxygen tags 4.4 sne 03/01/19 Fixed violations according to MISRAC-2012 standards modified the code for below violations, No brackets to then/else, Literal value requires a U suffix,Function return type inconsistent,Logical conjunctions need brackets, Declared the pointer param as Pointer to const, Procedure has more than one exit point. 4.4 sne 03/04/19 Added Support for Versal. 4.5 sne 09/27/19 Updated driver to support WWDT and AXI Timebase WDT. 5.0 sne 01/31/20 Removed compare value registers write while configuring Generic Watchdog window. 5.0 sne 02/27/20 Reorganize the driver source. 5.0 sne 03/09/20 Fixed MISRA-C violations. 5.7 sb 07/12/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 5.12 vlt 01/28/26 Fixed codespell issues.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 12/17/15 First release. 4.0 sha 01/29/16 Updated version to 4.0 as it is newly added file in driver version 4.0. 4.3 srm 01/30/18 Added doxygen tags 5.7 sb 07/12/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 5.12 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
